recent_trades.summary: Exiting ISRG on recent price weakness, -3.8% unrealized loss and only mild sentiment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: News explicitly questions whether to reconsider ISRG after share price weakness while sentiment is just +0.10; this position underperforms the portfolio's tech winners. Proceeds will help fund increases in NVDA and COST to better align with current AI/consumer momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to COST on very strong +0.48 sentiment, sales growth and membership model strength, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Headlines highlight April sales, e-commerce gains and valuation support near the $1000 level; minor -0.23% dip today is not concerning given positive coverage. Increasing from 5.75% deploys cash into a defensive name with proven resilience and only -0.4% unrealized loss.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NVDA on +0.32 sentiment, AI chip surge news and sector momentum, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA gained 0.47% today with multiple headlines reinforcing AI growth and refocus on chip stocks; current 2.01% weight and 5.4% unrealized gain leave room to scale to 4% without concentration risk. This builds on the ongoing semiconductor rally visible across AMD, INTC and QCOM.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing QCOM on bullish article suggesting 70% rally may be just start and strong semiconductor momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM has strong upward momentum (+0.86% today, near 30-day high) and a highly favorable article calling for continued gains. The chip sector is surging, and QCOM is well-positioned in mobile and AI. Adding to 4.5% weight fits within risk limits and is funded by the PG sale.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting PG to reallocate capital to higher-growth opportunities in semiconductors, given negative sentiment and underperformance.
recent_trades.reasoning: PG is a consumer staples name with no positive news catalyst, a slight unrealized loss of -2.38%, and neutral-to-negative sentiment. Selling frees up ~1.5% of portfolio value to redeploy into QCOM, which has strong technical momentum and a very bullish article suggesting further upside.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming ABBV from 5% to 3% on negative sentiment (-0.18) turning more cautious, freeing capital for higher-conviction opportunities, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV sentiment has turned negative at -0.18 today. While I recently added to ABBV on strong Q1 results, the negative sentiment shift warrants a trim. The Skyrizi narrative remains intact per SeekingAlpha, so I'm not exiting fully — just reducing from 5% to 3% to free capital. The recent buy was at 201.24 and current price 204.57 gives a small profit on the trim.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to Broadcom on strong AI-chip headlines and a constructive chart that is holding near the top of its recent range.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has multiple supportive AI/custom-chip headlines and positive sentiment, and the price is stable near the upper end of its 30-day band without looking overstretched versus peers. Increasing from 5.09% to 6.00% keeps sizing disciplined while leaning further into one of the cleanest AI trend exposures in the portfolio.

recent_trades.summary: Closing Intuitive Surgical because the position is very small and the chart remains weak near the 30-day low despite only modestly positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG is just 0.46% of assets and has the weakest technical profile in the book, sitting at the low end of its 30-day range after underperforming. The news flow is mildly positive but not strong enough to justify keeping a tiny capital allocation when stronger momentum opportunities exist elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Abbott because the position is tiny, news is only neutral, and the stock is hovering near its 30-day low with limited momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABT is only 0.48% of the portfolio, so it contributes little diversification or upside, and the recent headline mix is mixed-to-neutral rather than a fresh catalyst. Technically it is trading near the bottom of its recent range, so freeing this capital for stronger relative-strength names is a cleaner use of the trade budget.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL position on negative sentiment despite recent price strength, reallocating to higher-conviction names.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL carries the weakest sentiment (-0.14) with mixed big-tech headlines and no fresh positive catalysts since our earlier entry at lower cost basis; the apparent green candle today offers an exit point to free capital for LLY/MRK while staying within 50-trade daily limit.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small position in Merck on strong positive sentiment (+0.50) and a clinical trial collaboration with KEYTRUDA, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MRK has a sentiment score of +0.50 (positive) with headlines about a clinical trial collaboration and strong earnings quality. The stock is near its 30-day high (112.59) with elevated volume. I am buying with available cash (~$2,895) to achieve a 2.3% weight, as this is a new position with a clear catalyst but limited upside in the near term. I do not trim any existing positions as they are generally performing well.

recent_trades.summary: Starting Eli Lilly as a healthcare growth balance to the tech-heavy book on very strong sentiment and relative strength, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY shows the strongest healthcare sentiment in the feed, with growth-screen validation and supportive income-oriented headlines, while its chart appears firm near the upper end of the recent range. Initiating a 5% position adds sector diversification and quality growth exposure without spending too much of the trade budget on a first print.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Amazon on the strongest sentiment in the portfolio and continued AI-investment headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN has the best news tone among current holdings, with bond financing explicitly tied to AI expansion and broader AI ecosystem headlines reinforcing the growth narrative. The position is already working with a solid unrealized gain, and increasing it from 8.40% to 12% stays well below concentration limits while leaning into one of the cleanest fundamental stories.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Home Depot because the stock is soft near the session low and lacks a strong fresh catalyst, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: HD is one of the larger positions at 10.46% but today's news flow is only mildly positive and less compelling than the AI and healthcare names in the book. The chart panel shows early weakness versus several peers, so reducing the weight to 6% frees capital from a low-momentum holding without fully exiting a still-defensive consumer name.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AbbVie from 7.7% to 5% to reduce exposure to an underperforming asset and free up capital for high-momentum trades.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV continues to lag the broader market, trading down 0.3% intraday and sitting at a 3.3% unrealized loss. By reducing our weight to 5%, we rotate capital out of stagnant defensive positions and into stronger growth setups.

recent_trades.summary: Opening a 4% position in AMD to capture broad semiconductor momentum as it rallies 3.2% intraday.
recent_trades.reasoning: Semiconductors are demonstrating exceptional strength today, with Intel making record highs and creating a favorable tailwind for the entire sector. AMD is up 3.2% on solid volume, and this new allocation increases our exposure to the leading market theme.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in American Express based on highly positive sentiment, peer earnings beats, and strong institutional backing.
recent_trades.reasoning: AXP has registered a very high sentiment score of +0.68 today following Affirm's earnings beat and continued praise from Berkshire Hathaway. With no current financials exposure after exiting JPM earlier, establishing a 5% position diversifies our holdings into a fundamentally strong credit name.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Intel to 3% weight on record-high momentum and positive chip sector tailwinds from Apple and memory stocks.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel has been hitting record highs with strong 30-day performance (+8.64%) and positive semiconductor sector sentiment. The news highlights Intel's fourth straight record high. Increasing from ~2% to 3% weight to capture continued momentum while staying within risk limits.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AXP allocation to 10% on very positive +0.68 sentiment, Buffett praise and steady financials performance, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AXP shows the strongest sentiment in the universe today with direct Buffett endorsement headlines and credit-card growth news; current 7% weight with near-flat unrealized P/L allows room to add using freed cash, enhancing financials exposure while respecting position-size limits.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% INTC position on +8.6% breakout and chip-sector rally fueled by Apple report, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC is leading S&P 500 gainers with record highs, elevated volume and multiple AI/data-center headlines; sector momentum from AMD, AVGO and NVDA supports entry at close, targeting a modest 5% weight well below the 20% cap using ABBV sale proceeds.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting remaining ABBV position to reallocate capital on low +0.07 sentiment and slight underperformance relative to sector leaders.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV carries a -1.46% unrealized loss with only modest positive news flow around biotech awards and unrelated health policy items; full exit frees ~4% of portfolio for higher-conviction names showing stronger technical breakouts and sentiment without repeating the prior trim.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to American Express on very strong sentiment and a stable chart that still sits below recent highs, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AXP has the strongest sentiment in the current set (+0.68) with supportive headlines around Berkshire's continued affinity for the stock and payments-related momentum. Technically it is consolidating around 316 after pulling back from the 30-day high near 320, which offers a cleaner entry than chasing extended tech. I am increasing the weight from 6.03% to 8.0%, a moderate add that respects the late-day timing and preserves flexibility.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD after the sharp run pushed it near its 30-day high and late-session upside looks less attractive, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is up strongly over the past month (+3.17%) and the intraday chart shows a steep advance into 446-449 resistance with price close to the 30-day high of 449.00. News flow is only mildly positive and not a fresh company-specific catalyst, so this looks more like prudent profit-taking than abandoning the name. I am reducing the position from 3.78% to 3.0% to lock in gains while keeping exposure to semiconductor strength.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Adobe to a 6% weight on positive sentiment from a new healthcare collaboration and strong 2.1% intraday technical performance.
recent_trades.reasoning: Adobe is breaking higher with a 2.1% gain today, supported by news of a strategic healthcare collaboration. The chart shows a strong reversal pattern, presenting a good opportunity to average up.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Amazon to capture upward momentum fueled by highly positive sentiment around its new supply chain services.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon maintains a very high sentiment score (+0.44) as analysts highlight the margin potential of its new supply chain initiatives. The technical setup remains resilient despite broader market chop, justifying a bump to an 8% weight.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing UnitedHealth to a 10% portfolio weight following a technical breakout and news of positive guidance updates.
recent_trades.reasoning: UnitedHealth is displaying excellent relative strength, up 1.1% on the session and clearing resistance. Strong fundamental catalysts, including reduced prior authorization requirements and raised guidance, support a larger allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Intel to reach an 8% weight as it surges over 8% intraday on continued momentum from Apple partnership reports.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel is staging a massive breakout today, continuing its run to a fourth straight record high on huge volume. Increasing the position size capitalizes on this extraordinary momentum and positive market sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Caterpillar exposure from 10% to 5% to lock in minor gains and reduce industrial exposure amidst intraday weakness.
recent_trades.reasoning: Caterpillar is dropping 1.3% today, signaling short-term exhaustion in its uptrend. Halving the position secures profits and reduces downside risk in an underperforming sector.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Abbott Laboratories to stop out of a losing position as technicals continue to deteriorate.
recent_trades.reasoning: The stock is down another 1.3% intraday and currently sits at a near 6% unrealized loss for the portfolio. Despite positive fundamental news, price action remains firmly bearish, warranting a full exit.

recent_trades.summary: Selling GE Aerospace completely to cut losses amid technical weakness and a sharp intraday rejection.
recent_trades.reasoning: GE is down 1.7% today and the short-term chart illustrates a consistent series of lower highs and lower lows. Exiting now prevents further drag on the portfolio.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting JPMorgan to preserve capital as technicals break down intraday and the stock drops 1.7%.
recent_trades.reasoning: JPMorgan is showing marked relative weakness today with a 1.7% drop that breaks the 20-period SMA on the composite chart. With financials underperforming, capital is better deployed into stronger sectors.

recent_trades.summary: Boosting UnitedHealth to 5% on positive regulatory change eliminating prior authorization and strong fundamentals.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH recently announced elimination of prior authorization for 30% of services, a major catalyst. The stock is at a 30-day high with positive sentiment (+0.21) and strong earnings momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Amazon on positive sentiment (+0.44) and long-term growth narrative in AI and supply chain.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN shows strong positive sentiment and is a generational wealth builder according to analysts. The stock has stable technicals near its 30-day high, and the new supply chain services could drive margin expansion.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing American Express to 4% on very positive sentiment (+0.68) and strong analyst backing, including Buffett's endorsement.
recent_trades.reasoning: AXP has the highest sentiment score in the portfolio (+0.68), backed by positive analyst notes and a Berkshire Hathaway vote of confidence. The stock is near a 30-day high with solid volume, supporting further upside.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting PepsiCo small position to consolidate into stronger growth stories.
recent_trades.reasoning: PEP is a small position (1.48%) with minimal unrealized loss and no recent positive headlines. Exiting frees capital for higher-conviction names with stronger sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Comcast on neutral sentiment and no near-term catalysts, reallocating to higher-conviction names.
recent_trades.reasoning: CMCSA has neutral sentiment (-0.02), down -0.59% unrealized, and lacks positive news catalysts. The position is underperforming and capital can be better deployed elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming ABBV from 7.5% to 4% on lower +0.07 sentiment and slight underperformance.
recent_trades.reasoning: News mentions are indirect (Tempus AI, antidepressants) with less direct catalyst than healthcare AI names like ISRG; reduces exposure to rebalance toward higher momentum names while locking partial gains.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to COST on very positive +0.48 sentiment, strong valuation, and dividend appeal, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Defensive consumer staple with stable charts near highs, positive news on long-term returns versus peers like McDonalds; increasing from 4% weight as portfolio rebalance into reliable compounder with minimal downside.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMZN to 12% target on +0.44 sentiment and generational wealth builder headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: New supply chain and memory-chip momentum headlines reinforce AI/cloud tailwinds not fully priced in 30D performance; building on recent catalyst while keeping below 20% cap as core long-term holding.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting TMO to reallocate from -1.81% unrealized loss position amid only modestly positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Wells Fargo lowered PT and news flow is less compelling than AI leaders; technicals show recent underperformance versus peers, freeing 4.85% capital for higher-conviction reallocations without repeating recent trades.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining Intel stub after the outsized run has faded intraday and there is no fresh company-specific catalyst, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC is still up strongly over 30 days but down from its recent peak and the intraday chart shows a sharp earlier surge followed by flat-to-weaker consolidation near the highs. News sentiment is effectively neutral and prior trims already reflected the stock being extended; with only a 0.50% weight left, fully exiting simplifies the book and frees a small amount of capital without materially changing portfolio exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% CSCO position on +1.3% gain, +0.41 sentiment and multiple AI data center/infrastructure headlines.
recent_trades.reasoning: CSCO breaking above recent range with positive volume; Cramer and market reports highlight its role in AI cooling/servers, providing diversification into networking within the tech basket.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% AMD position on +3.3% outperformance today and +0.43 sentiment tied to chip rally from Apple and Intel news.
recent_trades.reasoning: Candles show strong uptrend with higher highs above 20-period SMA; news links AMD favorably in semiconductor surge, new position sized conservatively under 20% cap to capture AI momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting DE to reallocate as it declined 0.4% today with only modest positive sentiment despite agtech headlines.
recent_trades.reasoning: Small 2% position with -0.45% 30D return and recent candlesticks showing distribution; exiting unlocks capital for stronger momentum plays without violating position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting entire META position to free capital as it shows only neutral +0.04 sentiment and lacks momentum relative to AI peers.
recent_trades.reasoning: META is down 0.5% today with flat 30D performance and no standout headlines; reallocating to higher-conviction names maintains portfolio discipline while locking in near-breakeven.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Adobe to 3% on positive sentiment (+0.21) and a new healthcare collaboration that could drive adoption, despite YTD decline.
recent_trades.reasoning: ADBE shows positive news from a healthcare partnership, which may open new revenue streams. The stock is near its 30-day high, indicating momentum. This is a contrarian play on a beaten-down name with a catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Cisco to 4% on strong positive sentiment (+0.41) and AI infrastructure growth narrative, leveraging recent price strength (+0.55% 5D).
recent_trades.reasoning: CSCO has very positive sentiment from AI data center demand and upcoming earnings. The stock is trending up with volume support. This adds exposure to a key AI theme.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting T-Mobile US due to neutral sentiment and slight underperformance (-1.46% unrealized), despite insider buying, the stock lacks immediate catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: TMUS has been flat to down with no strong news tailwind. The insider buy is a positive but not enough to warrant holding given the opportunity cost.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Abbott Labs entirely as the stock continues to decline (-2.74% unrealized) with no positive sentiment catalysts, reallocating to names with stronger outlooks.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABT has been a laggard with negative price action and no recent positive headlines. The healthcare sector has better opportunities elsewhere, such as ADBE.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting McDonald's fully due to persistent weakness (-2.98% unrealized) and no positive catalysts, freeing capital for higher-conviction plays.
recent_trades.reasoning: MCD has dropped nearly 3% in our portfolio and shows no near-term positive news or technical improvement. The consumer discretionary sector has mixed signals, and MCD lacks the momentum of other holdings.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.23%, Nasdaq +0.14%, Dow +0.06%. Commodities led sectors (+2.60%) while Communication Services lagged (-0.81%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +2.15%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +22.20%. Key headline: ‘It’s shameful’: New York’s elite lash out at Zohran Mamdani’s second-home tax - Financial Times — market reaction muted.
